response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates strong general software engineering expertise across 30+ years, his career is fundamentally rooted in full-stack web, mobile, and application-layer development. The Principal Performance Architect role requires specialized expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, and deep-learning workload optimization—none of which are evidenced in his resume. His recent AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq focuses on agentic workflows and developer tooling, not hardware optimization. The domain mismatch is too significant for a principal-level position where deep, specialized hardware expertise is foundational rather than learnable on the job.
strengths: 30+ years of professional engineering experience with demonstrated commitment to system design and performance optimization across multiple domains
Proven ability to architect solutions across diverse technical stacks and layers, from embedded systems to distributed microservices
Recent exposure to AI infrastructure and willingness to work across emerging technology areas
concerns: Zero demonstrated experience with SoC design, IP modeling, or chip-level architecture—core competencies for this principal role
Career trajectory is application and web-layer focused; no background in GPU architecture, AI accelerators, or hardware-software co-design at the infrastructure level
Missing prerequisite expertise in SIMD/MIMD architecture and deep neural network optimization that the role explicitly requires

key_factors.factor: SoC/IP modeling and chip design experience
General systems architecture and performance optimization background
